property_preferences.suburbs.answer: Devonport, Takapuna, Milford, Browns Bay (all on Auckland's North Shore, ideally within walking or biking distance to the beach and close to good primary and intermediate schools). I’m also open to Bayswater or Belmont if the street is quiet and family-friendly.

property_preferences.property_type.answer: Free-standing family home (standalone house) with a small garden. I’m not interested in apartments or townhouses unless they have very good soundproofing, a bit of outdoor space, and low body corporate fees. Ideally a character home (1920s–1960s bungalow or villa) that has been modernised, but I’m also open to newer builds if they are well insulated and not part of a massive development.

property_preferences.preferred_land_size.answer: Approximately 400–700 m² section. Big enough for a small lawn, veggie garden, and a safe play area for our 5-year-old, but not so large that it becomes unmanageable with full-time work. A flat or gently sloping site is strongly preferred; we’d like to avoid very steep driveways or sections as we often have grandparents visiting.

property_preferences.other_requirements_.answer: We are a young family (two adults and a 5-year-old) looking for a warm, dry, and well-insulated home with good natural light and at least one secure off-street car park (a garage or carport would be ideal). Proximity to public transport (bus routes to the CBD) is important as I commute into central Auckland 3–4 days a week. We’d like to be within a 20–30 minute commute to the city off-peak, and no more than about 45–50 minutes during peak traffic if possible.

The property must be in-zone for a reputable primary school (e.g. Devonport Primary, Takapuna Primary, or similar). A quiet, family-oriented street with low traffic volume is crucial, as our son is learning to ride his bike. We’d prefer a home that doesn’t require major structural work (no obvious subsidence, major leaks, or unconsented additions). Some cosmetic updating (carpet, paint, kitchen/bathroom refresh) is fine and even welcome, as we’re happy to gradually improve the home over time. A separate space that can be used as a small home office would be a big plus, as I work from home 1–2 days a week and need a reasonably quiet area for video calls.

We are particularly interested in properties with good indoor-outdoor flow (e.g. living area opening onto a deck or patio), fully fenced or easily fenceable backyards for child safety, and reasonable storage (linen cupboard, wardrobes in bedrooms, and ideally some attic or under-house storage). We’d prefer properties that have had at least some modern upgrades such as heat pump(s), double-glazed windows (at least in bedrooms and living areas), and compliant insulation. We are open to both auctions and negotiation-based sales, but we would appreciate early access to LIM reports and building inspection reports where available, as we want to move relatively quickly (ideally purchasing within the next 3–6 months).
